violations.text: At the time of inspection, several food items in the Kitchen 2 door reach-in cooler were observed at 43 to 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Food items were moved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. (Food items cooling from previous preparation were observed in cooler near the items observed slightly above proper temperature. PIC informed cooling of hot food items is to take place in the walk-in cooler.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Food items were observed set for cooling in refrigeration in containers greater than four inches deep and/or covered in plastic wrap. PIC informed that when placed in cooling or cold holding EQUIPMENT, FOOD containers in which FOOD is being cooled shall be arranged in the EQUIPMENT to provide maximum heat transfer through the container walls, and loosely covered, or uncovered if protected from overhead contamination. Items were rearranged for proper cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

Bowls/single-use containers are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. items were removed from dry ingredients at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2025

The following equipment was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment... - At the time of inspection, the high temperature dish machine was observed to be properly sanitizing per thermo-testing device, but the pressure dial was not displaying the proper reading of 15-25 psi. Unit is to be serviced/adjusted for proper adjustment. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Prep unit external surfaces and gaskets - Shelving units Nonfood contact surfaces are to be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and preclude any build-up of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 03/08/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the prep area of the kitchen. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. (- A couple of light shields in the kitchen area were observed to be cracked/broken and in need of replacement.) New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025

At the time of inspection, a non-food-grade container (container with sharp angles, corners, crevices) was observed being used for holding of a dry food item (chips). Multiuse FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES shall be... - Smooth - Free of breaks, open seams, cracks, chips, inclusions, pits, and similar imperfections - Free of sharp internal angles, corners, and crevices Chips were moved to food-grade container at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2024

At the time of inspection, wooden bowl(s) used for dispensing/plating of chips was observed to be chipped/cracked and no longer in smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. Cracked/chipped bowls to be removed from service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/22/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Crab Rangoons were placed on the shelving racks in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC moved items to shallow pans and into refrigeration to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients (tempura flakes, peanuts, sesame seeds)are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following observed with: 1) Ice machine guard observed with biofilm 2) The handle of the knife in the kitchen observed with duct tape Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/12/2023
